title: Unable to reply to forums windows 10 since installing Firefox 148
content:
Hi,

Since installing the latest FF update (148) a few days ago, I have trouble replying to any topic on this site: https://www.4x4community.co.za/forum/. Prior to this update, there was no problem in replying or posting to the forum.

I do not have this problem on any of the other forums of which I am a member, only this one. I can log in and read posts with no problem, but I cannot reply to any post. I am also unable to start a new post on this particular forum (of which I am a Moderator). There is no flashing cursor when I wish to post, and I also cannot 'copy & paste' a reply either.

I have disabled Malware Bytes, Dark Reader and AdBlock plug-ins, this makes no difference.

If, however, I run FF in the "Troubleshoot" mode, I am able to to post replies to this forum. I have reinstalled FF v148 twice, the issue remains.

Testing the same forum with Opera browser I can access and reply to the forum above, even though with Opera I use the same plug-ins mentioned above turned on. It appears to thus be a FF permissions issue. I have cleared my Cookies and cache but this did not help.

Do you have any other suggestions? I am a FF user from day 1 all those years ago and have no desire to change to another browser.

Regards, Eric
creator: northcom

content:

The Firefox profile is copied over and then the version of the program from that other computer is copied, so the profile and program match. The last step is to disable each add-on individually, then to immediately enable them again. (Otherwise, the browser won't connect to the internet.)

I followed the same procedure today as every single time. But then I noticed the blank new tab add-on isn't working. Where did they go, and how can they be reinstated?

This has never happened before, and I've been doing this same thing for at least 3 years.

Thanks in advance.

title: PiP/Picture-In-Picture Freezes the Whole Browser
content:
Recently, whenever I put a video in PiP, the whole browser freezes. I'm not sure what causes it. The PiP player itself is playing and working fine, it's the browser as a whole that freezes completely. I can play/pause and even pop it back to its tab, but when I do, the browser is inaccessible to me because it freezes, despite the video still playing normally. I can only terminate the browser with Task Manager, otherwise the video will continue playing and the Firefox process goes on the background.

I have tried Googling for a way to fix it, but nothing seems to work. Some of the recommendations were to turn Hardware Acceleration off, allow autoplay, upgrade GPU drivers, and test in Troubleshoot Mode. I have autoplay completely off (including in about:config) and gave an exception to YouTube. I don't think this is the problem though, because I've had it set up this way for a long time, and the browser freezing only started happening recently. I have had Hardware Acceleration off for a while too, so it's not because of that. My GPU drivers are up-to-date (Nvidia). The browser still freezes in Troubleshoot Mode.

The problem is not limited to YouTube. Wherever I can pop the player off with PiP, it will freeze the whole browser (like Twitch or any other site with video player capabilities). I tried playing a video in a different browser and it worked just fine with no freezing, it only happens in Firefox.

I noticed that the common issue people report is the PiP player itself freezing, but my case is the exact opposite. The PiP plays fine while the rest of the browser freezes. I really like this feature and don't want to turn it off if possible. I like to pop the video out and watch it play while I scroll around on my desktop.

title: I, and tens of millions of other people, use Grok as the AI chatbot. Why is it not included among the AI available?
content: I have found that Grok is more likely to provide a balanced answer than chatGPT, which usually swings to the left, and has a tendency to just make stuff up. I haven't really tried the rest on your list, I haven't even heard of some of them, for that matter. I don't understand why you couldn't include it, when you included all the rest. While I'm at it, why did you remove the upvote/down-vote in the new tab suggested sites? I was down-voting all of the far-left progressive garbage, out of tens of thousands of pages you never showed a single right-wing suggestion from sites like Fox, New York Post, etc., or even the center, like The Hill, but once I got rid all of the garbage from Huffington, Salon, Vox, Guardian, CNN, there were some good articles by sites like AP, Smithsonian, Scientific American, etc. that showed up and were center or center-left news, not propaganda. I'm giving up on the tab suggestions for now, I'll check it again sometime to see if you are still ignoring the 32% of the population who are right-wing Republican, and the about 38% of the population who are independent/center, to continue to concentrate on the 30% who are left-wing Democrats, and more particularly the tiny percentage who are Progressives among the Democrats. Instead of increasingly becoming the browser of choice for just one part of the population, it might be a good idea to think about representing the other 70% of the population as well.

title: Firefox rendering glitch: Jittery scrolling and image flickering on high-res industrial galleries.
content:
Hi everyone,

I am experiencing a persistent rendering issue on my company's website when viewing it through Firefox (v123.0+). The site features heavy-duty industrial units and detailed technical specification tables, which involve several high-resolution PNGs and large CSS grids.

The Issue: When scrolling through our product gallery, the images seem to "flicker" or disappear momentarily. This does not happen in Chrome or Edge, which leads me to believe it’s a Firefox-specific rendering bug related to hardware acceleration or the way Firefox handles large object-fit: cover containers.

Live URL for testing: [https://portaking.pk/|https://portaking.pk/]

Details:

Flickering: Occurs primarily on the "Porta Cabin" product pages where we use an image slider.

Console Errors: I am seeing a "Layout Force Reflow" warning in the Firefox Console that I can't seem to replicate in other browsers.

Troubleshooting: I have tried disabling all extensions and clearing the cache, but the jittery scrolling persists on this specific layout.

Is there a known CSS conflict between Firefox’s rendering engine (Gecko) and modern WordPress grid layouts that might be causing this? I want to make sure our clients using Firefox have a smooth experience.

Thanks for any insights!
creator: portakingofficial

title: I turned on my computer this morning and it seems my password file has disappeared. Has Firefox made this change, and if so, why?
content: I use Stockhouse.com as a homepage and when I opened my computer this a.m. the site came up and it asked me to sign in which it normally does these days and I click on sign in and it shows my email and password and I click again and I'm signed in. This morning it asked me to provide my email address and password i.e. fill in the blanks. It has not done that for at least 15 - 20 years. Not remembering offhand what my password was I clicked on the 3 line application tab in the top right hand of my screen and brought up the list which includes "Passwords". I clicked on the password file and it is blank. It has never been blank before -- did Mozilla/Firefox make a change that would make my password file disappear?
creator: veitchw
